channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Tinubu Admits Stubbornness in Early Administration Days During Iftar with Journalists
item.description: President Bola Tinubu don confess say he was small stubborn when him govt just start. Him talk this one during interfaith breaking of fast with senior journalists and media executives for Presidential...

item.title: Izak Rankine prepares for first AFL match since suspension
item.description: Adelaide Crows forward Izak Rankine is set to return to AFL action after serving a suspension for using a homophobic slur. Rankine spoke with 9News ahead of his comeback, expressing enthusiasm and det...

item.title: Analysis of US–Africa Health Funding Agreements and Associated Risks
item.description: In late February 2026, the United States signed health funding agreements worth $19.8 billion with 18 African countries, including Nigeria, Kenya, and South Africa. The US committed $12.2 billion, wit...

item.title: Kenya's Cancer Agency Requests Additional Sh250 Million to Boost Prevention and Early Detection
item.description: The National Cancer Institute of Kenya has asked Parliament for an extra Sh250 million to address key gaps in cancer prevention, early detection, and diagnosis. Acting CEO Elias Melly told MPs that th...

item.title: Meningitis B outbreak in Kent reaches 15 cases with two deaths; targeted vaccination planned for university students
item.description: An outbreak of invasive meningococcal disease, primarily the MenB strain, has affected 15 individuals in Kent, southern England, with all cases requiring hospital admission and two young people sadly ...

item.title: Pakistan Day celebrations to be scaled down due to national austerity measures
item.description: On March 17, 2026, the Government of Pakistan announced that the annual Pakistan Day parade and other ceremonial events scheduled for March 23 will not be held this year. This decision comes as part o...

item.title: STEMpower Installs 159th STEM Centre at TCNPC in Dodoma, Second in Tanzania
item.description: STEMpower has successfully set up its 159th Science, Technology, Engineering, and Mathematics (STEM) centre at the Tanzania Community Networks Polytechnic College (TCNPC) in Dodoma. This new centre ma...

item.title: Pakistan Day to be observed with simple flag hoisting amid Gulf oil crisis
item.description: The federal government of Pakistan has announced that the traditional Pakistan Day parade and associated ceremonial events will not be held this year on March 23, due to austerity measures linked to t...

item.title: Renovated Carlton North Terrace Blends Heritage Charm with Modern Design
item.description: Tom Tyrrell, born in Carlton in the 1940s, has spent decades immersed in Melbourne's inner northern suburbs. He witnessed the evolution of the area from working-class, European-influenced communities ...

item.title: KTR raises concern over Speaker’s decision to dismiss disqualification petition against Danam Nagender
item.description: On March 17, 2026, the issue of the disqualification petition against Khairatabad MLA Danam Nagender resurfaced in the Telangana Assembly. The petition, filed by the Bharat Rashtra Samiti (BRS), quest...

item.title: Government Approves Comprehensive Reforms for Pakistan Agricultural Research Council
item.description: ISLAMABAD: Prime Minister Shehbaz Sharif has formally approved a major restructuring of the Pakistan Agricultural Research Council (PARC) to strengthen the country's agriculture sector and align it wi...

item.title: Sears AI Chatbot Customer Data Was Publicly Accessible Online
item.description: Sears, though largely absent from U.S. retail, continues to operate its appliance repair services with an AI chatbot named Samantha. Security researcher Jeremiah Fowler discovered three publicly acces...

item.title: Analysis of Iran's Drone and Missile Campaign Against UAE in 2026 Conflict
item.description: In the ongoing conflict involving Iran, Israel, and the United States in 2026, Iran has launched a large-scale retaliatory campaign using drones and missiles targeting the United Arab Emirates (UAE) a...

item.title: Police seek help to find two 14-year-olds missing from Feltham for ten days
item.description: Hillingdon Police have issued a public appeal for assistance in locating two 14-year-old teenagers, Ethan and Morgan-Leigh, who have been missing from Feltham, west London, since 8 March 2026. The tee...

item.title: Examining Paraxanthine: A Potential Alternative to Caffeine in Beverages
item.description: Paraxanthine, a naturally occurring compound produced when the body metabolises caffeine, is emerging as a possible alternative stimulant in energy drinks and some coffee products. Proponents suggest ...

item.title: PM Shehbaz commends Kazakhstan President on adoption of new constitution
item.description: Prime Minister Shehbaz Sharif congratulated Kazakhstan President Kassym-Jomart Tokayev on the success of the recent constitutional referendum, calling it a historic step. The referendum, held on Monda...

item.title: FCT High Court fines EFCC N500,000 for delaying Godwin Emefiele’s trial
item.description: For Maitama, Abuja, the Federal Capital Territory High Court don give EFCC gbege as dem don award N500,000 cost against the anti-graft agency. This one happen because EFCC dey delay the trial of forme...

item.title: Khaby Lame Sells Digital Rights While Balancing Faith and Fame
item.description: Khaby Lame, born Khabane Lame in Dakar, Senegal, has become TikTok's most followed creator with over 160 million followers. Known for his silent, expressive reactions to overly complicated 'life hacks...

item.title: Sam Mitchell addresses player behaviour and club culture following US arrest of Hawthorn stars
item.description: Hawthorn coach Sam Mitchell faced intense questioning regarding the culture at his club after the off-season arrest of star players Dylan Moore and Connor Macdonald in the United States. During an app...

item.title: NIT urged to improve innovation and attract more students for global competitiveness
item.description: Dar es Salaam: The Parliamentary Standing Committee on Infrastructure has called on the National Institute of Transport (NIT) to enhance its innovation and promotional strategies to attract more stude...
